docs.title: TraceAPI
docs.text: TraceAPI is a FastAPI service for 3D neuron tracing. It wraps bundled neuTube and Vaa3D APP2/smartTrace binaries, accepts uploaded TIFF volumes or server-side TIFF paths, and returns SWC reconstructions.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• neuTube tracing for uploaded or server-side volumes.
  • Vaa3D APP2 and smartTrace tracing.
  • Directional subtree extraction from ordered seed pairs s1 -> s2.
  • Batch seed-pair support: one SWC for a single pair, ZIP for multiple pairs.
  • Temporary run outputs under runs/, cleaned periodically by the service.

docs.title: Repository Layout
docs.text:
app.py             FastAPI routes
app_helpers.py     upload, workdir, subprocess, and volume-prep helpers
subtree_utils.py   seed parsing and swclib-based subtree extraction
vaa3d_utils.py     Vaa3D/SWC utility functions
algorithms/        bundled neuTube, Vaa3D, Qt libraries, and plugins
docs/              MkDocs documentation source
mkdocs.yml         MkDocs Material configuration

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • Vaa3D/Qt system libraries
  • Python packages: fastapi, uvicorn, python-multipart, numpy, scipy, tifffile, swclib

Install common system dependencies:

sudo apt-get install -y xvfb libglu1-mesa \
  libqt5widgets5 libqt5gui5 libqt5xml5 libqt5network5 libqt5core5a libqt5concurrent5

Install Python dependencies:

pip install fastapi uvicorn python-multipart numpy scipy tifffile
pip install -e /path/to/swclib

docs.title: Run Locally
docs.text:
Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1920x1080x24 -ac &
export DISPLAY=:99
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$PWD/algorithms:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"
python -m uvicorn app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --workers 4

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/trace_vaa3d_app2_subtree \
  -F "tif_path=/data2/public_data/CWMBS/image/SN21.tif" \
  -F "s1=242.8440,249.8640,7.9980" \
  -F "s2=243.5728,248.0015,7.9980" \
  --output SN21_seed1_app2.swc

See API Reference for all endpoints.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
Base URL for local service examples:

http://127.0.0.1:8000

The GitHub Pages URL is documentation-only:

https://yang-ze-kang.github.io/TraceAPI/

docs.title: Common Inputs
docs.text:
Supported image suffixes:

.tif, .tiff, .png, .jpg, .jpeg

Tracing routes expect 3D image volumes.

Subtree routes accept seed points as a single pair:

s1=242.8440,249.8640,7.9980
s2=243.5728,248.0015,7.9980

or multiple pairs:

s1=10,20,30;40,50,60
s2=11,20,30;42,50,60

JSON arrays are also accepted:

s1=[[10,20,30],[40,50,60]]
s2=[[11,20,30],[42,50,60]]

Single seed-pair routes return one .swc; multiple seed pairs return subtrees.zip.

docs.title: POST /trace_neutube
docs.text:
Run neuTube tracing on an uploaded volume.

Name Required Type Description file yes file Input volume.

Returns output.swc.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/trace_neutube \
  -F "file=@/path/to/volume.tif" \
  --output output.swc

docs.title: POST /trace_neutube_subtree
docs.text:
Run neuTube tracing, then extract directional subtrees from seed pairs.

Name Required Type Description file one of file/tif_path file Uploaded input volume. tif_path one of file/tif_path string Server-side input path. s1 yes string Start seed point(s). s2 yes string Direction seed point(s).

Returns subtree.swc for one pair, or subtrees.zip for multiple pairs.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/trace_neutube_subtree \
  -F "tif_path=/data2/public_data/CWMBS/image/SN21.tif" \
  -F "s1=242.8440,249.8640,7.9980" \
  -F "s2=243.5728,248.0015,7.9980" \
  --output SN21_seed1_neuTube.swc

docs.title: POST /trace_vaa3d_app2
docs.text:
Run iterative Vaa3D APP2 tracing on an uploaded volume.

Name Required Type Description file yes file Input volume.

Returns output.swc.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/trace_vaa3d_app2 \
  -F "file=@/path/to/volume.tif" \
  --output app2.swc

docs.title: POST /trace_vaa3d_app2_subtree
docs.text:
Run Vaa3D APP2 from each s1 marker, then apply the same filter_swc_subtree directional extraction used by neuTube subtree tracing.

Name Required Type Description file one of file/tif_path file Uploaded input volume. tif_path one of file/tif_path string Server-side input path. s1 yes string APP2 marker/start seed point(s). s2 yes string Direction seed point(s).

Returns subtree.swc for one pair, or subtrees.zip for multiple pairs.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/trace_vaa3d_app2_subtree \
  -F "tif_path=/data2/public_data/CWMBS/image/SN21.tif" \
  -F "s1=242.8440,249.8640,7.9980" \
  -F "s2=243.5728,248.0015,7.9980" \
  --output SN21_seed1_app2.swc

docs.title: POST /trace_vaa3d_smartTrace
docs.text:
Run iterative Vaa3D smartTrace tracing on an uploaded volume.

Name Required Type Description file yes file Input volume.

Returns output.swc.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/trace_vaa3d_smartTrace \
  -F "file=@/path/to/volume.tif" \
  --output smarttrace.swc

docs.title: Subtree Extraction
docs.text:
For each ordered pair s1 -> s2:

  1. Find the closest SWC node to s1.
  2. Reroot the traced tree at that node.
  3. Resample with spacing equal to distance(s1, s2).
  4. Find the child branch closest to the target direction.
  5. Remove other root branches and return the subtree.

docs.title: Serve the API
docs.text:
Start an X display for Vaa3D, configure shared library paths, and run FastAPI with Uvicorn:

Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1920x1080x24 -ac &
export DISPLAY=:99
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$PWD/algorithms:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"
python -m uvicorn app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --workers 4

docs.title: Serve the Documentation Locally
docs.text:
Install MkDocs Material:

pip install mkdocs-material

Preview the documentation:

mkdocs serve

Build the static site:

mkdocs build

docs.title: Publish to GitHub Pages
docs.text:
Deploy to the repository's gh-pages branch:

mkdocs gh-deploy --force

After deployment, the documentation is served at:

https://yang-ze-kang.github.io/TraceAPI/

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The GitHub Pages site is static documentation only.
  • The FastAPI service must run on a server with access to the tracing binaries and input data.
  • tif_path values are resolved on the API server, not in the browser.
